gaps: The bug is about a BRIEF/MOMENTARY popup that appears upon app LAUNCH (not navigation to a settings screen). No existing test covers the app-launch-triggered popup behavior for Charge Case firmware updates.

No test validates the timing/duration of the popup ('briefly appears'). This is a transient UI behavior that could be missed if the popup auto-dismisses too quickly or appears only during a specific lifecycle phase.

No test covers the specific state: app launch → Charge Case on outdated firmware → popup appears momentarily. Existing tests focus on language-change-triggered DFU or manual navigation to Software Update screens.

LIFECYCLE GAP: No test validates what happens when the app is backgrounded (home button) and then foregrounded again — does the popup reappear? Does it only show once per app session? This is a critical lifecycle persistence gap.

LIFECYCLE GAP: No test validates what happens when the app is force-killed and relaunched — does the popup appear again on relaunch? This tests whether the 'already shown' flag persists across app restarts.

LIFECYCLE GAP: No test validates what happens when Bluetooth disconnects and reconnects — does the popup reappear after reconnection? This tests whether the popup is tied to the connection event or the app launch event.

DEVICE ASYMMETRY GAP: The bug is specific to Voyager Free 60+ (which has a touchscreen charge case). No existing test validates the Charge Case firmware popup behavior specifically for this device model vs. other models like Voyager Free 20 (which lacks the touchscreen case).

STATE TRIGGER GAP: No test validates the exact trigger condition — does the popup appear because the Charge Case firmware version is checked at app launch, or because of a delayed BLE service discovery? The 'momentary' nature suggests a race condition that is not tested.
No test validates the user interaction with this popup — can the user tap it? Does it auto-dismiss? What happens if the user is mid-interaction when it appears? These UX flow gaps are untested.

generated_testcases.title: VF60+_ChargeCase_Popup_AppLaunch_Transient_Appearance
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected, Charge Case firmware is outdated (e.g., v1.0.0 vs latest v2.0.0).
Force-kill Poly Studio Mobile app.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Observe the screen immediately upon app launch (within first 3 seconds).
Note: Do not navigate anywhere — stay on the initial connected device screen.
generated_testcases.expected: A brief/momentary popup about Charge Case firmware update appears within 2-3 seconds of app launch. Popup auto-dismisses after ~2-3 seconds without user interaction. Popup text references 'Charge Case' firmware (not earbud firmware). Actual (If Bug): Popup appears for <500ms and is unreadable, or does not appear at all despite outdated Charge Case firmware.

generated_testcases.title: VF60+_ChargeCase_Popup_AppLaunch_UserDismiss_Interaction
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected, Charge Case firmware outdated.
Force-kill and relaunch Poly Studio Mobile app.
When the Charge Case firmware popup appears, tap on it (or tap the 'Update' / action button if present).
Observe navigation destination.
If popup auto-dismisses before tap, note the auto-dismiss timing.
generated_testcases.expected: Tapping the popup navigates to the Software Update / DFU screen for the Charge Case. If popup auto-dismisses before user can tap, it must remain visible for at least 2 seconds. Actual (If Bug): Popup dismisses in <1s making it untappable, or tapping it does nothing, or tapping it navigates to earbud firmware update instead of Charge Case firmware update.

generated_testcases.title: VF60+_ChargeCase_Popup_Lifecycle_Background_Foreground
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected, Charge Case firmware outdated.
Force-kill and relaunch app.
Observe popup appears momentarily and auto-dismisses.
Press Home button to background the app.
Wait 10 seconds.
Re-launch app (bring to foreground via app switcher or tapping icon).
Observe if popup reappears.
generated_testcases.expected: Popup does NOT reappear when app is foregrounded from background. The 'already shown' flag persists across background/foreground transitions within the same app session. Actual (If Bug): Popup reappears on every foreground transition, causing repeated disruptive UI flashes.

generated_testcases.title: VF60+_ChargeCase_Popup_Lifecycle_ForceKill_Relaunch
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected, Charge Case firmware outdated.
Launch app, observe popup appears and auto-dismisses.
Force-kill the app (swipe from recents).
Wait 5 seconds.
Relaunch the app.
Observe if popup appears again.
generated_testcases.expected: Popup DOES appear again on relaunch after force-kill. The 'already shown' flag is NOT persisted across app restarts — each fresh launch triggers the firmware version check. Actual (If Bug): Popup does not appear on relaunch (stale flag persists), or popup appears multiple times within the same launch cycle.

generated_testcases.title: VF60+_ChargeCase_Popup_Lifecycle_Bluetooth_Reconnect
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected, Charge Case firmware outdated.
Launch app, observe popup appears and auto-dismisses.
Disconnect Bluetooth from phone Settings (or turn off earbuds).
Wait for app to show 'Disconnected' state.
Reconnect Bluetooth (turn on earbuds, wait for auto-reconnect).
Observe if popup reappears upon reconnection.
generated_testcases.expected: Popup does NOT reappear upon Bluetooth reconnection within the same app session. The popup is tied to the app launch event, not the Bluetooth connection event. Actual (If Bug): Popup reappears on every Bluetooth reconnect, causing repeated popups during unstable connection scenarios.

generated_testcases.title: VF60+_ChargeCase_Popup_Lifecycle_Bluetooth_Reconnect_AfterForceKill
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected, Charge Case firmware outdated.
Launch app, observe popup appears and auto-dismisses.
Force-kill the app.
Disconnect Bluetooth (turn off earbuds).
Turn on earbuds, wait for auto-reconnect.
Launch app.
Observe if popup appears.
generated_testcases.expected: Popup appears on app launch regardless of Bluetooth reconnection state. The trigger is app launch + outdated Charge Case firmware, not the connection event. Actual (If Bug): Popup does not appear because the reconnection event races ahead of the app launch check, or popup appears twice (once from stale connection event + once from launch check).

generated_testcases.title: VF60+_ChargeCase_Popup_DeviceAsymmetry_VF20_NoPopup
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 (non-touchscreen case) connected, earbud firmware is current but no Charge Case firmware concept exists.
Force-kill and relaunch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Observe the screen immediately upon app launch.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update.
Verify no Charge Case firmware update option exists.
generated_testcases.expected: No Charge Case firmware popup appears on app launch for Voyager Free 20. The Software Update screen shows only earbud firmware update options. Actual (If Bug): A Charge Case popup appears for VF20 (which has no touchscreen case), or the popup incorrectly references earbud firmware as 'Charge Case' firmware.

generated_testcases.title: VF60+_ChargeCase_Popup_StateTrigger_RaceCondition_BLEServiceDiscovery
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected, Charge Case firmware outdated.
Enable Bluetooth HCI Snoop Log on phone (developer options).
Force-kill and relaunch app.
Capture the exact timestamp of: (a) app process start, (b) BLE service discovery completion for Charge Case, (c) popup appearance, (d) popup dismissal.
Repeat 5 times.
Analyze timing deltas between (a)→(c) and (b)→(c).
generated_testcases.expected: Popup appears consistently within 2-3 seconds of app launch across all 5 runs. Popup appearance correlates with app launch (a) not with BLE service discovery completion (b). Timing delta (a)→(c) should be <3000ms. Actual (If Bug): Popup appearance is erratic — sometimes appears at 500ms, sometimes at 8s, or not at all — indicating a race condition with BLE service discovery timing.

generated_testcases.title: VF60+_ChargeCase_Popup_StateTrigger_UpToDateFirmware_NoPopup
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected, Charge Case firmware is already up-to-date (latest version).
Force-kill and relaunch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Observe the screen immediately upon app launch for 10 seconds.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update.
Verify Software Update screen shows 'Up to date' for Charge Case.
generated_testcases.expected: No Charge Case firmware popup appears on app launch when firmware is current. Software Update screen confirms Charge Case is up to date. Actual (If Bug): Popup appears even when firmware is current (false positive), or popup appears but references incorrect version information.

generated_testcases.title: VF60+_ChargeCase_Popup_StateTrigger_MidInteraction_Overlay
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected, Charge Case firmware outdated.
Force-kill and relaunch app.
Immediately upon app launch, start tapping rapidly on the device settings area (e.g., tap 'Audio Settings' repeatedly).
Observe if the popup interrupts the interaction.
Note whether the tap passes through the popup or is consumed by it.
generated_testcases.expected: Popup appears as an overlay that consumes taps while visible — taps do not pass through to underlying UI elements. After popup auto-dismisses, the underlying UI becomes interactive again. Actual (If Bug): Taps pass through the popup (causing unintended navigation), or the popup blocks all UI permanently after dismissal, or the popup causes a touch event to be lost/dropped.

generated_testcases.title: VF60+_ChargeCase_Popup_StateTrigger_AppAlreadyOpen_NoPopupOnReconnect
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected, Charge Case firmware outdated.
Launch app, observe popup appears and auto-dismisses.
Keep app open and in foreground.
Turn off earbuds (disconnect Bluetooth).
Wait for app to show 'Disconnected' state.
Turn on earbuds (reconnect Bluetooth).
Observe if popup reappears while app remains continuously open.
generated_testcases.expected: Popup does NOT reappear upon Bluetooth reconnection while the app remains continuously open in the foreground. The popup is triggered only once per app session at launch. Actual (If Bug): Popup reappears on every reconnection event, causing repeated interruptions during the same app session.
